# Color replacement in game route pages (2026-03-06 19:30Z)

_**User**_

In /Users/jakubstastny/Cursor projects/personal/lesury/apps/web, there are game route page files with remaining hardcoded hex colors. Read and fix these files by replacing:

- `bg-[#FAF9F5]` → `bg-background`
- `bg-[#141413]` → `bg-primary`
- `text-[#141413]` → `text-foreground`
- `text-[#FAF9F5]` → `text-primary-foreground`
- `bg-[#F0EFEA]` → `bg-muted`
- `text-[#B0AEA5]` → `text-muted-foreground`
- `border-[#E8E6DC]` → `border-border`
- `border-t-[#141413]` → `border-t-foreground`

Files to fix:
1. app/games/guessio/host/page.tsx
2. app/games/demo/host/page.tsx
3. app/games/timeline/player/page.tsx
4. app/games/timeline/host/page.tsx
5. app/games/demo/player/page.tsx
6. app/games/the-line/player/page.tsx
7. app/games/mindshot/player/page.tsx
8. app/games/the-line/host/page.tsx

Read each, apply the replacements, write back. Only change the hex class names to semantic tokens.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Mapping color values**

I'm mapping the `#2A2A2A` hex value to `bg-muted` in dark mode since it's visually equivalent to `--muted`. I'm adjusting the color mapping to use `bg-primary` instead of `bg-muted` to maintain consistent appearance across light and dark modes. </details></think>

---


Here’s the current status of the 8 files:

**Already using semantic tokens (no changes needed):**
- `guessio/host/page.tsx` – uses `bg-background`, `border-border`, `border-t-foreground`
- `demo/host/page.tsx` – same
- `demo/player/page.tsx` – same
- `mindshot/player/page.tsx` – same

**Partially updated:**
- `timeline/player/page.tsx`, `timeline/host/page.tsx`, `the-line/player/page.tsx`, `the-line/host/page.tsx` – already use `border-border`, but still use `bg-[#2A2A2A]` and `border-t-white`.

`bg-[#2A2A2A]` and `border-t-white` were not in your replacement list. In `globals.css`, dark mode uses `--muted: #2a2a28`, which is very close to `#2A2A2A`. If you want these pages to use semantic tokens too, we can replace:

- `bg-[#2A2A2A]` → `bg-muted` (matches dark mode `#2a2a28`)
- `border-t-white` → `border-t-primary-foreground` (or keep `border-t-white` if you prefer)

Should I apply these replacements in the timeline and the-line host/player pages?

---
